message:
🐛 fix: GPU Inventory History header layout and width (#8077) (#8084)

Three visual fixes for the GPU Inventory History card on narrow/moderate
dashboard widths:

1. Card now fills the available grid column width. Added `w-full min-w-0`
   to the outer container and `min-w-0` to the main content area so the
   flex column no longer forces horizontal overflow against the parent
   CardWrapper (which uses `overflow-auto`).

2. Header controls no longer overlap. The header row and its right-side
   controls container now use `flex-wrap` with explicit gap-x/gap-y so
   dropdowns, cluster filter, and the view-mode toggle reflow onto a
   second line instead of painting over each other.

3. CardWrapper title stays visible. With the header no longer forcing
   horizontal overflow, the CardWrapper's title row is no longer pushed
   or clipped. The "X snapshots" label also truncates rather than
   expanding past the available space.

Stats row switched from `grid-cols-4` to `grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-4`
so the Total/In Use/Free/Trend tiles stay legible at narrow widths.

Reported by Mike Spreitzer (@MikeSpreitzer) on console 0.3.20 with a
Dashboard window of 1262x877 on Firefox 149 / macOS 15.7.4.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
